title: EnterpriseBench Expected Solution
description: Curated ground truth consumed by the LLM judge in lib/eb_verify/runner.py. One file per task, alongside task.toml and ground_truth.json. Every checkpoint declared in the task's [[checkpoints]] table must have a corresponding entry here — see scripts/validation/validate_expected_solutions.py for the structural and coverage gates.

task_id.description: Must equal the `id` field in this task's task.toml [task] table.

checkpoints.description: Map keyed by checkpoint name. Keys MUST exactly match the `name` of every [[checkpoints]] entry in task.toml — extra or missing keys are validation errors.

expected_solution.description: Long-form prose describing the canonical solution. Function-level and file-path-specific. Used by the LLM judge as the reference against which the agent's submission is scored.

evaluation_criteria.description: Concrete, machine-evaluable assertions about the agent's submission. Each criterion is a free-form string but should reference specific files, functions, or causal-chain steps. Minimum 2 entries; high-weight (>0.30) checkpoints want >=3.

_curation_required.description: Set by scripts/validation/scaffold_expected_solution.py when it cannot mechanically populate this checkpoint. Files containing this flag fail validation — operators must hand-curate before shipping.
